Lionel Messi ( In-depth ).notes:
Lionel Andrés Messi is an Argentine professional footballer, widely regarded as one of the greatest players in the history of the sport. Born on 24 June 1987 in Rosario, Santa Fe Province, he currently captains Major League Soccer club Inter Miami and the Argentina national team.

**1. Personal Life and Early Career**

*   **Family Background:** Messi is the third of four children born to Jorge Messi, a steel manufacturer, and Celia Cuccittini, who worked in a magnet manufacturing workshop. His parents met in Rosario's Las Heras district, where Messi grew up. On his father's side, he is of Italian and Spanish descent, with his surname originating from immigrants from Porto Recanati in Italy's Marche region. His mother's side is primarily Italian.
*   **Childhood and Early Football:** Growing up in a tight-knit, football-loving family, Messi developed a passion for the sport from an early age, constantly playing with his older brothers, Rodrigo and Matías, and cousins, Maximiliano and Emanuel Biancucchi (both of whom became professional footballers).
    *   At age four, he joined local club Abanderado Grandoli FC, coached by his father.
    *   His maternal grandmother, Celia, was a significant figure, accompanying him to training and matches. Her death, shortly before his eleventh birthday, deeply affected him. As a devout Catholic, Messi celebrates his goals by looking up and pointing to the sky in her tribute.
    *   At age seven, he began playing for Rosario-based Newell's Old Boys, becoming a lifelong supporter. During his six years there, he scored almost 500 goals as a member of "The Machine of '87," a nearly unbeatable youth team. He often entertained crowds with ball tricks during half-time. His goalscoring idol was Brazilian striker Ronaldo, whom he called "the best forward I've ever seen."
*   **Growth Hormone Deficiency and Move to Barcelona:**
    *   At age 10, Messi was diagnosed with a growth hormone deficiency, threatening his future as a professional player. He began growth hormone therapy at age 11, which cost at least 1,000 pesos per month. His father's health insurance covered only two years, and Newell's reneged on a promise to contribute.
    *   He was scouted by Buenos Aires club River Plate, where he idolized playmaker Pablo Aimar. River Plate reportedly offered to pay for his medical treatment after he scored four goals at a trial, but Newell's refused to release his player card, preventing the transfer.
    *   With relatives in Catalonia, the Messi family arranged a trial with Barcelona in September 2000, when Messi was 13. First-team director Carles Rexach immediately wanted to sign him, but the board hesitated due to the unusual nature of signing such a young foreign player. On 14 December, an ultimatum led Rexach to famously write a contract on a paper napkin.
*   **Integration into La Masia:**
    *   In February 2001, Messi's family relocated to Barcelona, moving into an apartment near Camp Nou.
    *   During his first year, he rarely played with the Infantiles due to a transfer conflict with Newell's, only being eligible for friendlies and the Catalan league as a foreigner.
    *   He struggled to integrate, being reserved by nature, with some teammates initially believing he was mute. He also suffered from homesickness after his mother, brothers, and younger sister, María Sol, moved back to Rosario. Messi stayed in Barcelona with his father.
    *   After a year at Barcelona's youth academy, La Masia, he was finally enrolled in the Royal Spanish Football Federation (RFEF) in February 2002, allowing him to play in all competitions. He quickly befriended teammates like Cesc Fàbregas and Gerard Piqué.
    *   After completing his growth hormone therapy at 14, Messi became an integral part of the "Baby Dream Team," Barcelona's greatest-ever youth side. In his first full season (2002–03), he was the top scorer with 36 goals in 30 games for the Cadetes A, who won an unprecedented treble (league, Spanish cup, Copa Catalunya). In the Copa Catalunya final, he played with a protective mask after a broken cheekbone, took it off, and scored two goals in 10 minutes, a game referred to as "partido de la máscara."
    *   He received an offer from Arsenal but chose to remain in Barcelona, unlike Fàbregas and Piqué who left for England.

**2. Club Career**

*   **Barcelona (2004–2021):**
    *   **Rapid Ascent:** Messi progressed rapidly through Barcelona's ranks, debuting for four youth teams in the 2003–04 season. After excelling in pre-season tournaments, he was promoted to Juvenil A, scoring 18 goals in 11 league games. He was called up to the depleted first team, with winger Ludovic Giuly describing him as an "alien" who "destroyed us all" in training.
    *   **First Team Debut and Early Success:**
        *   At 16, Messi made his first-team debut in a friendly against FC Porto on 16 November 2003. His performance impressed the technical staff, leading him to train daily with Barcelona B and weekly with the first team. Ronaldinho, Barcelona's star player, called him "little brother" and predicted he would surpass him.
        *   He signed his first professional contract on 4 February 2004, lasting until 2012 with an initial buyout clause of €30 million.
        *   Messi made his La Liga debut for Barcelona on 16 October 2004, at 17 years old, becoming the youngest player to represent the club in an official competition at the time.
        *   He scored his first senior goal on 1 May 2005, becoming Barcelona's youngest-ever scorer. Barcelona won the league title that season.
        *   On his 18th birthday, he signed his first senior team contract through 2010 with a €150 million release clause. After an impressive performance against Juventus in the Joan Gamper Trophy, Inter Milan offered to pay his buyout clause and triple his salary. Barcelona responded by updating his contract, doubling his salary and extending it to 2014. He acquired Spanish citizenship on 26 September 2005, making him eligible to play fully.
        *   He established himself as the first-choice right winger, forming an attacking trio with Ronaldinho and Samuel Eto'o. Barcelona won La Liga and the UEFA Champions League in the 2005–06 season, though Messi missed the Champions League final due to a torn hamstring.
    *   **Rise to Global Stardom:**
        *   In the 2006–07 season, at 19, Messi scored his first hat-trick in a Clásico against Real Madrid, the first in 12 years. His goal against Getafe in the Copa del Rey, where he ran 60 meters beating five defenders, was later voted Barcelona's best goal ever.
        *   After a trophyless 2007–08 season, manager Frank Rijkaard departed. Upon Ronaldinho's departure in 2008, Messi was given the iconic number 10 shirt.
        *   Under new manager Pep Guardiola, Messi became the tactical focal point, leading to a significant increase in his goalscoring rate. In 2008–09, he scored 38 goals and, alongside Samuel Eto'o and Thierry Henry, contributed to a club-record 100 goals. Barcelona achieved the first treble in Spanish football history, winning the Copa del Rey, La Liga, and the Champions League (Messi scored in the final against Manchester United). He was the youngest Champions League top scorer.
        *   In 2009, he won his first Ballon d'Or and FIFA World Player of the Year by the largest voting margin in history. Barcelona went on to win the Supercopa de España, UEFA Super Cup, and FIFA Club World Cup, becoming the first club to achieve the sextuple.
    *   **Dominance and Record-Breaking:**
        *   Messi won four consecutive Ballon d'Ors from 2009 to 2012.
        *   In the 2011–12 season, he set the European record for most goals in a season (73 goals in all competitions) and became Barcelona's all-time top scorer at 24, surpassing César Rodríguez's 57-year record. He also set a La Liga record with 50 goals in a single season.
        *   In 2012, he broke Gerd Müller's record for most goals in a calendar year, scoring an astonishing 91 goals for club and country.
        *   During the 2014–15 campaign, Messi became the all-time top scorer in La Liga, surpassing Telmo Zarra's 59-year record. He led Barcelona to a historic second treble, becoming the first club to achieve this feat, with the attacking trio of Messi, Suárez, and Neymar (MSN) scoring a record 122 goals in Spanish football. He won his fifth Ballon d'Or in 2015.
        *   He assumed Barcelona's captaincy in 2018 after Andrés Iniesta's departure, lifting his first trophy as captain, the Supercopa de España, and later his tenth La Liga title. He won a record sixth Ballon d'Or in 2019.
        *   Messi continued to break records, becoming the first player to score 40+ goals in 10 consecutive club seasons and the first in La Liga to score 20+ goals in 12 consecutive seasons. He surpassed Xavi's record for most appearances for Barcelona (768) in the 2020–21 season and led the club to victory in the 2021 Copa del Rey final.
        *   He finished his last two seasons with Barcelona as La Liga's top scorer, earning a record-breaking eight Pichichi Trophies. He also surpassed Pelé's record for most goals scored for a single club (672).
    *   **Departure from Barcelona:**
        *   After his contract expired on 1 July 2021, negotiations for a new deal were complicated by Barcelona's severe financial difficulties and La Liga regulations. Barcelona announced that Messi would not stay, leading to a tearful press conference where he confirmed his departure. He left as the club's all-time leader in appearances, goals, assists, and trophies (a club-record 34 trophies, including ten La Liga titles and four UEFA Champions Leagues).

*   **Paris Saint-Germain (2021–2023):**
    *   On 10 August 2021, Messi joined Ligue 1 club Paris Saint-Germain (PSG) on a free transfer, signing a two-year deal with an option for an extra year and reuniting with Neymar. He chose the number 30, the same number he wore as a teenager at Barcelona.
    *   He made his debut for PSG against Reims and scored his first goal for the club in a Champions League group stage win over former manager Pep Guardiola's Manchester City.
    *   In his debut season (2021–22), he helped PSG win their 10th Ligue 1 title, finishing with 11 goals and 14 assists across all competitions.
    *   In the 2022–23 season, he won his second trophy with PSG, the Trophée des Champions. He surpassed Cristiano Ronaldo as the all-time highest goalscorer in European club football with 702 goals and achieved 1,000 career direct goal contributions at club level. He also became the player with the most goals in Europe's top five leagues (496).
    *   He finished the 2022–23 season with 21 goals and a league-high 16 assists, helping PSG clinch their 11th Ligue 1 title, his second in a row. Manager Christophe Galtier confirmed his departure after the season.

*   **Inter Miami (2023–Present):**
    *   On 15 July 2023, Major League Soccer (MLS) club Inter Miami CF announced Messi's signing on a two-and-a-half-year contract. His arrival in the US was hailed as a revelation, sparking "Messimania" and significantly raising the profile of MLS. His No. 10 jersey became the best-selling in the league and nearly worldwide.
    *   He made his debut on 21 July 2023 in a Leagues Cup match against Cruz Azul, scoring a dramatic free-kick in stoppage time for a victory. He was given the captain's armband by previous captain DeAndre Yedlin.
    *   After scoring nine goals in his first six games, Messi led Inter Miami to win their first-ever trophy, the 2023 Leagues Cup, where he was named best player and top scorer with ten goals.
    *   He made his MLS debut on 26 August 2023, scoring a late goal against the New York Red Bulls, ending Miami's 11-match league winless streak.
    *   In the 2024 season, Messi broke the record for most assists (5) and most goal contributions (6) in a single MLS game against the New York Red Bulls. He scored his first hat-trick for Inter Miami on 19 October 2024 against the New England Revolution, helping the club finish with a league-record 74 points and clinch the Supporters' Shield. He finished the 2024 regular season with 20 goals and 16 assists in 19 matches, becoming Inter Miami's all-time top goalscorer. He was named MLS Most Valuable Player for 2024.
    *   In the 2025 season, Messi became the fastest player to reach 40 goals in league history. He won the MLS Golden Boot as the league's top scorer with 29 goals and 19 assists in 28 games. On 23 October 2025, he signed a three-year contract extension with Inter Miami through 2028.
    *   He led Miami to MLS Cup 2025, where they defeated the Vancouver Whitecaps 3–1 to win the club's first league championship. Providing two assists in the final, he was named MLS Cup MVP. He set a new record for most goal contributions in a single MLS postseason (15 – 6 goals, 9 assists). He was again named MLS MVP, becoming the first player to win the award in back-to-back years.
    *   As of 1 March 2026, Messi scored his 70th career free-kick goal, drawing him level with Pelé. His career total of 405 assists (as of November 2025) surpassed Ferenc Puskás for the most career assists of all time.

**3. International Career - Argentina**

*   **Youth and Senior Debut:**
    *   As a dual Argentine-Spanish national, Messi was eligible for both countries but chose Argentina. He debuted for Argentina's U20 team in 2004, leading them to victory in the 2005 FIFA World Youth Championship, where he won the Golden Ball as best player and Golden Boot as top scorer (6 goals, 2 assists). This was his first success with Argentina.
    *   He won a gold medal at the 2008 Summer Olympics with Argentina's U23 team, registering two goals and three assists.
    *   Messi made his senior national team debut at age 18 in a friendly against Hungary on 17 August 2005, but was controversially sent off after two minutes. He scored his first international goal in a friendly against Croatia on 1 March 2006.
*   **Early World Cup Appearances and Captaincy:**
    *   He made his FIFA World Cup debut in 2006, assisting one goal and scoring the final one in Argentina's 6–0 victory over Serbia and Montenegro, making him the youngest player to represent and score for Argentina in the World Cup.
    *   Messi represented Argentina in the 2007 Copa América, where they lost in the final, and he was named the best young player of the tournament.
    *   Assuming the number 10 shirt after Juan Román Riquelme's retirement, Messi first wore the captain's armband in a 2010 FIFA World Cup group stage match. Argentina was eliminated in the quarter-finals by Germany, with Messi failing to score but providing one assist.
    *   Following an unsuccessful 2011 Copa América campaign, a 24-year-old Messi was awarded the captaincy of the squad.
*   **Finals Heartbreak and Retirement:**
    *   The next several years were marked by frustrations as Messi led Argentina to three consecutive finals losses: the 2014 World Cup final (to Germany), the 2015 Copa América final (to Chile on penalties), and the 2016 Copa América Centenario final (again to Chile on penalties, where Messi missed his penalty). Despite these losses, he was awarded the Golden Ball as the best player of the 2014 World Cup and was named in the team of the tournament for the 2015 and 2016 Copa América. In the 2016 Copa América Centenario semi-final, he surpassed Gabriel Batistuta as Argentina's all-time leading goalscorer.
    *   After the 2016 Copa América Centenario final, Messi initially announced his international retirement at age 29.
*   **Return and International Triumphs:**
    *   A nationwide campaign in Argentina convinced Messi to reverse his decision. He returned to help his country narrowly qualify for the 2018 World Cup with a crucial hat-trick against Ecuador. Argentina was eliminated in the round of 16 by France, with Messi contributing one goal and two assists.
    *   **Ending the Drought:** Messi was central to ending Argentina's 28-year trophy drought by winning the 2021 Copa América, their first international title since 1993. He was named player of the tournament, scoring four goals and assisting five, being directly involved in nine of Argentina's twelve goals. This victory helped him secure his seventh Ballon d'Or that year.
    *   **World Cup Glory:** In the following year, Messi captained Argentina to win the 2022 Finalissima against UEFA Euro 2020 winners Italy, providing two assists and being named player of the match. Later that year, he led Argentina to winning the 2022 FIFA World Cup, their first in 36 years. He scored twice in the final against France and, with seven goals and three assists throughout the tournament, again won the Golden Ball, becoming the first player to win it twice. His appearance in the final set new records for most appearances (26) and most goal contributions (21) in the World Cup. He also became the first player to score in every round of a single World Cup.
    *   **Continued Success:** Messi followed this achievement with a record-extending eighth Ballon d'Or in 2023. He was victorious at the 2024 Copa América, his third major international title as captain, despite suffering a severe ankle injury in the final. He set a new record for most appearances (39) at the Copa América.
    *   **International Records:** Messi is Argentina's all-time leading goalscorer (115 goals) and most-capped player (196 caps). He is also the top assist-provider in men's international football history (61 assists as of November 2025). In 2023, he became the third player and first South American to reach 100 international goals and the all-time top goalscorer in CONMEBOL World Cup qualifiers (36 goals).

**4. Playing Style and Attributes**

*   **Physicality and Agility:** Due to his short stature, Messi possesses a lower center of gravity, granting him exceptional agility to change direction quickly and evade tackles. The Spanish media dubbed him "La Pulga Atómica" ("The Atomic Flea"). Despite his physically unimposing size, he has significant upper-body strength and balance, allowing him to withstand physical challenges. He is also noted for his lack of diving. His short, strong legs enable bursts of acceleration and quick feet for ball control at speed, leading Pep Guardiola to famously state, "Messi is the only player that runs faster with the ball than he does without it."
*   **Technical Skills:** Predominantly left-footed, Messi uses the outside of his left foot for dribbling runs and the inside for finishing, passes, and assists. He is a prolific goalscorer known for his finishing, positioning, quick reactions, and ability to make attacking runs. He also functions as a creative playmaker, renowned for his vision and range of passing, often described as a "magician" or "conjurer" who creates opportunities where none seem to exist. He is an accurate free-kick and penalty-kick taker, ranking third all-time in goals from direct free kicks (69 as of July 2025) and holding the most among active players. His pace and technical ability allow him to undertake individual dribbling runs, particularly during counterattacks, often starting from the halfway line or the right side of the pitch. He is widely considered one of the greatest dribblers of all time, with Diego Maradona remarking, "The ball stays glued to his foot; I've seen great players in my career, but I've never seen anyone with Messi's ball control."
*   **Tactical Evolution:** Beyond individual qualities, Messi is a well-rounded, hard-working team player, known for creative combinations, especially with former Barcelona midfielders Xavi and Andrés Iniesta. As his career progressed, his athleticism and tendency to dribble diminished slightly with age, leading him to dictate play from deeper areas and develop into one of football's best passers and playmakers. His work-rate off the ball and defensive responsibilities also decreased, allowing him to conserve energy for short bursts of speed, improving his efficiency, movement, and positional play, and reducing muscular injuries.
*   **Positional Versatility:** Tactically, Messi plays in a free attacking role, capable of attacking on either wing or through the center. His childhood favored position was the "enganche" (playmaker behind two strikers). He began his career in Spain as a left-winger but was moved to the right wing by Frank Rijkaard, allowing him to cut inside and curl shots with his left foot. Under Guardiola, he most often played in a "false nine" role, positioned as a center-forward but roaming deep into midfield to draw defenders and create space. Under Luis Enrique, he initially returned to the right wing but was increasingly deployed in a deeper, free playmaking role. Under Ernesto Valverde, he played various roles, including deeper runs, right wing, false nine, offensive central in a 4–2–3–1, or as a second striker in a 4–4–2. With the Argentina national team, he has similarly been employed across the frontline, from right wing to false nine, out-and-out striker, supporting forward, or a deeper creative number 10 playmaker.

**5. Awards and Recognition**

*   **Individual Accolades:** Messi is the most decorated player in the history of professional football, with 46 team trophies and numerous individual accolades.
    *   **Ballon d'Or:** He has won a record eight Ballon d'Or awards (2009, 2010, 2011, 2012, 2015, 2019, 2021, 2023), the most for any footballer. He is the only player to win it in three different decades and in four consecutive years.
    *   **FIFA Awards:** He has been named the world's best player by FIFA eight times (FIFA World Player of the Year/FIFA Ballon d'Or/The Best FIFA Men's Player in 2009, 2010, 2011, 2012, 2015, 2019, 2022, 2023).
    *   **Golden Shoes:** He has won a record six European Golden Shoes (2009–10, 2011–12, 2012–13, 2016–17, 2017–18, 2018–19).
    *   **World Cup Awards:** He is the only player to win the FIFA World Cup Golden Ball more than once (2014, 2022).
    *   **Other Major Awards:** He has won the Laureus World Sportsman of the Year twice (2020, 2023), becoming the first team-sport athlete to do so. In 2023, he was named Time's Athlete of the Year, the first footballer to receive this honor. In 2025, he was named the All Time Men's World Best Player by the IFFHS. He has also won the MLS Most Valuable Player award in back-to-back years (2024, 2025).
    *   **Records:** Messi holds numerous world records, including most (recorded) Man of the Match awards (+400), most goals scored for a single club (672 for Barcelona), most goals in a calendar year (91 in 2012), most assists in football history (407), and most goal contributions in football history (1,305).
*   **Team of the Year Selections:** He has been included in the FIFPRO World 11 a record 17 times (2007–2023) and the FIFA/EA Sports FC Team of the Year 16 times (2008–2023). He has also been named to the Copa América Team of the Tournament six times and the UEFA Team of the Year multiple times.

**6. Rivalries and Comparisons**

*   **Cristiano Ronaldo:** Messi is widely regarded as one of the two best players of his generation, alongside Portuguese forward Cristiano Ronaldo. Their ongoing rivalry is often compared to legendary sports rivalries. While Messi is lauded for his dribbling, playmaking, passing, and goalscoring, Ronaldo is praised for his speed, athleticism, goalscoring skills, and performance under pressure. Their differing physiques and public personalities also fuel the debate. In terms of individual accolades, Messi holds more Ballons d'Or (8 vs 5), FIFA World's Best Player awards (8 vs 5), and European Golden Shoes (6 vs 4). Their competitive matchups, particularly in El Clásico, were highly anticipated global events. Following Messi's 2022 World Cup win, many pundits and players opined that he had settled the debate, with Ronaldo himself declaring the rivalry "over and gone" after 15 years.
*   **Diego Maradona:** Throughout his career, Messi has been compared with his compatriot Diego Maradona, due to their short statures, similar left-footed playmaking styles, and Argentine origins. Maradona himself called Messi the best player in the world and his successor when Messi was 18. They worked together as player and manager for Argentina from 2008 to the 2010 World Cup, during which Maradona gave Messi his famous number 10 shirt.
    *   Initially, Messi was held in lesser esteem in Argentina than Maradona due to his lack of senior international tournament success and his perceived reserved personality compared to Maradona's fiery, extroverted nature. Messi's development overseas in La Liga, rather than the Argentine Primera División, also contributed to this perception.
    *   However, Messi's conscious effort to become "more one of the group, more Argentine" from 2019 onwards, including singing the national anthem and being more vocal, began to change public perception. His victories in the 2021 Copa América and especially the 2022 FIFA World Cup, which Maradona had famously won in 1986, cemented his legacy in Argentina. Pundits noted a "Maradonian" edge to his 2022 World Cup performances, and Messi himself stated that the victory "won over all the people of Argentina."

**7. Off-Field Activities**

*   **Public Image and Influence:** Messi has been included in Time's 100 most influential people in the world in 2011, 2012, and 2023. A 2014 survey found him to be the second-most recognized player globally (after Ronaldo) and the most likeable. His social media presence is immense, with over 114 million Facebook followers and over 500 million Instagram followers (second only to Ronaldo). His 2022 World Cup celebration post is the most liked on Instagram, with over 75 million likes. His cultural impact is evident in street art, public murals, and statues erected in his honor worldwide, including a bronze statue in Buenos Aires and a 70-foot statue in Kolkata, India.
*   **Earnings and Endorsements:** Messi was the world's highest-paid footballer for five out of six years between 2009 and 2014 and was ranked the world's highest-paid athlete by Forbes in 2019 and 2022. In 2020, he became the second footballer and team-sport athlete (after Ronaldo) to surpass $1 billion in career earnings. His income largely derives from endorsements, with Adidas being his main sponsor since 2006. He has had a signature line of Adidas boots and was the first footballer to receive his own Adidas sub-brand. He has also endorsed brands like Pepsi, Konami (Pro Evolution Soccer), EA Sports (FIFA), Gillette, Turkish Airlines, Ooredoo, and Tata Motors. In 2021, he became an ambassador for Hard Rock Cafe, and in 2022, he was unveiled as Saudi Arabia's tourism ambassador, a role that drew criticism due to human rights concerns.
*   **Charitable Work:** Driven by his own childhood medical struggles, Messi is deeply involved in charitable efforts for vulnerable children. He has contributed time and finances to UNICEF since 2004 and has served as a UNICEF Goodwill Ambassador since 2010, undertaking field missions and participating in campaigns for HIV prevention, education, and social inclusion. In 2007, he founded the Leo Messi Foundation, which supports access to healthcare, education, and sport for children, financing medical training, research grants, and development projects globally. He has made significant donations, including €1 million to fight the spread of coronavirus and paying the salaries of national team security staff during an economic crisis. In 2025, he was bestowed with the Presidential Medal of Freedom by US President Joe Biden for his contributions to supporting healthcare and education programs for children.
*   **Business Ventures:** In 2024, Messi launched Más+, an American brand of sports and energy drinks. Later that year, he announced the creation of 525 Rosario, a production company named after his hometown, headquartered in Miami and Los Angeles, to produce film, sporting events, and branded commercials. In 2025, he was invited to participate in a new soccer project, Deportivo LSM, focusing on football training.
*   **Legal Issues:** In 2013, Messi's financial affairs came under investigation for suspected tax evasion, involving offshore companies in Uruguay and Belize to evade €4.1 million in taxes between 2007 and 2009. An unrelated shell company in Panama was also identified in the Panama Papers leak. Despite pleading ignorance, he voluntarily paid €5.1 million in arrears. In July 2016, Messi and his father were found guilty of tax fraud and handed suspended 21-month prison sentences, with Messi ordered to pay €1.7 million in fines.

**8. Personal Life (Continued)**

*   **Family:** Since 2008, Messi has been in a relationship with Antonela Roccuzzo, whom he has known since age five (she is the cousin of his childhood best friend, Lucas Scaglia). They married on 30 June 2017 in Rosario and have three sons: Thiago (born 2 November 2012), and two others born later. Messi and his family are Catholic.
*   **Family Business and Rosario Ties:** He maintains a close relationship with his immediate family, particularly his mother, Celia, whose face is tattooed on his left shoulder. His professional affairs are largely run as a family business, with his father, Jorge, acting as his agent since he was 14, his oldest brother, Rodrigo, handling his daily schedule and publicity, and his mother and other brother, Matías, managing the Leo Messi Foundation and personal matters in Rosario.
*   **Hometown Connection:** Despite living in Barcelona since age 13, Messi has maintained close ties to Rosario, preserving his distinct Rosarino accent. He keeps ownership of his family's old house, maintains a penthouse apartment for his mother, and a family compound just outside the city. He remains in daily contact with a small group of confidants, many from "The Machine of '87." Although he was on bad terms with Newell's after his transfer to Barcelona, the feud ended by 2012, and he has long planned to return to Rosario to end his playing career at his boyhood club.
*   **Citizenship:** Messi holds triple citizenship: Argentine, Italian, and Spanish.

**9. Career Statistics and Records (Summary)**

*   **Overall Totals:** As of March 2026, Messi has scored over 890 senior career goals for club and country and provided over 400 assists, making him the player with the most assists and over 1,300 goal contributions, the highest total in the sport's history.
*   **Trophies:** He has won a record 46 team trophies, making him the most decorated player in professional football history.
    *   **Club:**
        *   **Barcelona (34):** 10 La Liga, 7 Copa del Rey, 7 Supercopa de España, 4 UEFA Champions League, 3 UEFA Super Cup, 3 FIFA Club World Cup.
        *   **Paris Saint-Germain (3):** 2 Ligue 1, 1 Trophée des Champions.
        *   **Inter Miami (3):** 1 MLS Cup, 1 Supporters' Shield, 1 Leagues Cup.
    *   **International (Argentina, 5):** 1 FIFA World Youth Championship (U20), 1 Olympic Games (U23), 1 FIFA World Cup, 2 Copa América, 1 Finalissima.
